title: Bit Digital Deepens Ethereum and AI Infrastructure Push in Q1 2026
text: Bit Digital Inc reported Q1 2026 revenue of $27.9 million while significantly advancing its strategic shift toward Ethereum treasury management and AI infrastructure through its majority-owned subsidiary WhiteFiber. The company maintained approximately 155,444 ETH worth roughly $327 million as of March 31, 2026, based on an ETH price of approximately $2,104. However, the company's average acquisition cost stands at approximately $3,045 per token, indicating current positions are underwater. During the quarter, Bit Digital repositioned roughly 70,000 ETH into liquid-staked variations to maintain treasury flexibility while continuing to earn yield. Colocation services emerged as a standout performer, with revenue climbing 23.9% quarter-over-quarter to $4.8 million. Cloud services contributed $16.8 million in revenue, while Ethereum staking generated $2.3 million. The company demonstrated particularly strong margins in staking operations with approximately 94.7% gross margins. Cloud services achieved roughly 59.5% margins, while colocation reached approximately 59.3%. CEO Sam Tabar stated that Ethereum infrastructure will become increasingly important as stablecoins, tokenization, and on-chain settlement activity continue scaling globally.
